def: adv. To-day; now; (see āj)
subs: adyāvadhi, adv. Even to this day, still, even yet, even now.

def: adj. First, initial, prior, pre-eminent.
s.m. pl. (of دين dīn), Creeds; religions; religious rites and ceremonies.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَدْيَان (ʔadyān).

def: adj. Lustreless, without splendour. (See ajot.)
v.t. To assent or agree to, to admit, accept, be responsible for.
s.f. The head and foot of a bed; the upper and lower end (of anything), this side and that; — adj. & adv. Lower; beneath.
= āeṅtī-pāʼeṅtī, q.v.

ety: Zend āba, rt. bā; S. ābhā, rt. bhā
def: s.m. The sun; the musk-deer.
s.m. Serpent; viper; asp; basilisk.
s.f. An imitative sound (as of a dog barking), bow-wow.
s.f. An imitative sound (as of a dog barking), bow-wow.
s.m. pl. (of P. افغان afg̠ān), The people of Afgānistān, the Afgān race.
s.m. pl. (of افق ufq, or ufuq), Horizons; quarters of the heavens; quarters of the world; regions.
s.m. pl. (of فعل fěʻl), Actions, deeds; conduct.
s.m. pl. (of ʻifrīt), Wicked or malignant genii or spirits, horrible demons or giants.
s.f. Bane, pest, plague; any evil affection; evil, disaster, trouble, misfortune, calamity; wretchedness, misery, hardship, difficulty; (cf. S. āpat)
subs: (-par), āfat paṛnā (-par), āfat ṭūṭnā (-par), Calamity to befall, trouble or evil to come (upon); disaster, or a disturbance, to arise
v.n. To suffer pain, affliction, trouble, &c.; to bear up against adversity, difficulties, &c.; to raise trouble, difficulties, &c.; to create a disturbance; to inflict trouble
(or āfateṅ) ḍālnā, v.t. To interpose difficulties or troubles, to cause misfortune
āfat-zada, part. adj. & s.m. Overtaken by calamity, involved in misfortune; unfortunate, wretched; — unfortunate creature, poor wretch
s.m. A very wicked person; a sharp, astute fellow
s.f. Bad news, intelligence of a death; a talisman to work mischief
āfat-meṅ phaṅsnā, āfat-meṅ mubtilā honā, v.n. To be involved in misfortune, trouble, &c.; to be overwhelmed with misery, affliction, &c.; to be entangled in difficulties.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آفَت (āfat), borrowed from Arabic آفَة (ʔāfa).

def: s.f. pl. (of آفت āfat), Evils, troubles, misfortunes, calamities, dangers, &c. ( = āfateṅ, see āfat).
s.m. Lamentation, &c. = فغان fig̠ān, q.v. intj. Alas!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افغان (afġān).

def: s.m. A people (called also Paṭhāns), who inhabit the country to the N.W. of Lahore. (They pretend to derive their origin, as well as their name, from Afg̠ān, the reputed grandson of Malik Tālūt, or King Saul.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افغان (afġān).

ety: part. act. of afgandan, see afganda
def: adj. Casting, throwing down; casting forth (used in compn.).

r: afganda, afgandan, af, kantan, aibi, kan
ety: perf. part. of afgandan = af+Pehl. kantan = Zend aibi+rt. kan = S.अभि+rt. खन्
def: part. adj. Thrown, overthrown, cast forth.
s.f. Throwing, casting, overthrowing.
adj. Of or relating to the Afg̠āns; — s.f. The Afg̠ān language ( = Pushtū).
adj. Wounded; — s.m. A sore (on the back of a beast of burden).
adj. Abstinent, continent, chaste, virtuous, decent, modest, decorous; — ʻafīfa, s.f. A chaste woman, a modest woman, &c.

ety: for A. عفيفة, fem. of ʻafīf
def: adj. Abstinent, continent, chaste, virtuous, decent, modest, decorous; — ʻafīfa, s.f. A chaste woman, a modest woman, &c.
s.f. Opium.
s.m. Opium-eater; (fig.) A sot
s.m. Opium-eater; (fig.) A sot
s.m. Opium-eater; (fig.) A sot
s.f. Praise, applause; — part. adj. & s.m. Creating; creator (used in compn. e.g. jahān-āfirīn, Creator of the world); — intj. Bravo! well done!
subs: (-par), To pronounce āfrīn (upon), to praise, laud, applaud.

ety: for A. عافية, v.n. fr. عافا 'to grant health,' iii of عفو 'to efface,' &c.
def: s.f. Health, soundness; safety, security; well-being, welfare, freedom from evil or discomfort, &c.; success, prosperity.
s.f. pl. (of فکر fikr), Thoughts, meditations, opinions, counsels.
s.m. pl. (of فلك falak), The heavens, heavenly bodies; celestial orbs or spheres.
adj. Heavenly, celestial; relating to the heavenly bodies.
s.m. A person of overweening pride, an arrogant, disdainful man
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَفْلَاطُون (ʔaflāṭūn), from Ancient Greek Πλάτων (Plátōn).

def: s.m. pl. (of فرد fard), Single ones, single numbers, individuals; persons; sheets (of paper); separate sheets.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افراد (afrād), plural form of فرد (fard), from Arabic فَرْد (fard, “individual”).

def: adj. & s.m. European, Frank; — a Frank, a native of Europe.

ety: A. افراط ifrāt̤+تفريط tafrīt̤; root فرط
def: s.f. Hurry-skurry; confusion, disorder; bustle, tumult, commotion, uproar; alarm, consternation, panic; tumultuous dispersion, stampede.

ety: imperat. of afrāḵẖtan = af+rāḵẖtan; Zend aibi+rt. rag; S. abhi+langh
def: part. adj. Exalting, raising; extolling (used in compn.).

ety: perf. part of āfrīdan; ā+rt. Zend frī = S. प्री
def: part. adj. Created; — s.m. Creature.
s.m. The Creator.
part. adj. & s.m. Creating; — the Creator.

ety: abst. s. fr. āfrīn; ish = Zend she
def: s.f. Creation.

ety: perf. part. of afroḵẖtan = af+roḵẖtan; Zend aibi+rt. ruć; S. abhi+ruć
def: part. adj. Set on fire, lighted, kindled, inflamed.
adj. & s.m. Kindling, illuminating, enlightening; inflaming; kindler, &c. (used in compn.).
s.m. Gall (both the tree and the nut or fruit).
adj. compar. & superl. (of فصيح faṣīḥ), More, or most, eloquent; very eloquent; speaking very chastely or correctly.

ety: af = Zend aiwi or aibi = S. abhi; sāna, fr. Zend rt. śā = S. śā (śo)
def: s.m. Tale, fiction, story, romance
subs: s.f. Story-telling; the profession of a story-teller.

def: s.f. Wormwood, Artemisia absynthium.
s.m. Officer, official superior, one who exercises authority, a superior
subs: Superior officer, highest officer, chief authority
wety: Borrowed from English officer.

def: s.m.f. Crown, diadem.
wety: Borrowed from English officer.

ety: part. act. of afshāndan; af = Zend aiwi = S. abhi+caus. of rt. Zend shu = S.च्यु
def: part. adj. Scattering, strewing, dispersing, shedding, pouring out (used in compn., e.g. gul-afshān, Strewing roses; zar-afshān kāg̠aẕ, Paper sprinkled with gold); — s.f. ( = zar afshān), Strips of gold and silver leaf or tinsel, or threads of muqqaish (q.v.) chipped very fine, pasted as ornaments on the forehead or the cheeks of women, or on books, letters, &c.
adj. Sprinkled, scattered over, &c.; — s.f. Scattering, sprinkling, dispersion

ety: perf. part. of afshurdan; af = Zend aiwi = S. abhi+Zend rt. khshar = S. kshar
def: part. adj. Pressed, squeezed out; filtered.
s.m. Sorrow, grief, concern; regret; vexation; — intj. Ah! alas! alack! ah me! what a pity!
subs: afsos karnā (-kā, or par), To feel regret, sorrow, &c., to regret; to grieve, sorrow, or sigh (for, or because of), to lament, bemoan; to take to heart; to express sorrow or regret (for); to feel or to express pity (for).
ex: اُس کو کچھ افسوس نہیں ہوا۔
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افسوس (afsōs).

def: s.m. Incantation, charm, spell, verses used in spells or enchantments, fascination, sorcery, witchcraft
subs: (-par), A charm to work or take effect (upon)
(-par), To practise charms or incantations (on), work spells (on), to enchant, to exorcise, to conjure: (afsūn is combined, in the same sense, with paṛhnā, phūṅknā, ćalānā and mārnā)
afsun-sāz, s.m. One who uses spells, enchanter, sorcerer, wizard, magician
afsūn-sāzī, s.f. Enchantment, using of spells; bewitching, fascination; sorcery, &c. ( = afsūn).

ety: perf. part. of afsurdan; Zend aibi+śareta, rt. śar; S. abhi+rt. śar
def: part. adj. Frozen, frigid, benumbed; withered, faded; dispirited, dejected, low-spirited, melancholy
subs: adj. Depressed in mind, dispirited, afflicted — afsurda-dil, adj. Fainthearted, low-spirited, dejected.

def: s.f. Frozenness; frigidity, coldness; numbness; dejection, melancholy, lowness or depression of spirits.
s.m. (āf+tāb, q.q.v.), s.m. Lit. 'Sunshine'; the sun
subs: s.m. A worshipper of the sun
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آفْتَاب (āftāb). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi آفتاب (āftab /⁠ãftāb⁠/).

def: s.m. An ewer, water-pot ( = āb-tāba, q.v.).
adj. Of the sun; like the sun; solar; faded by the sun, yellow; — s.f. A parasol of a particular form; a target studded with gold; a shield made of tortoise-shell; a kind of firework.
s.m. = آفتابه āftābo, q.v.

ety: for A. ʻafwuṉ, inf. n. of عفو 'to efface, blot out,' &c.
def: s.m. Absolution, forgiveness, pardon, remission (of sins)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عَفْو (ʕafw).

def: s.m. (pl. of فوه fūh), Lit. 'Mouths'; report, rumour, hearsay, notoriety, fame
subs: To spread a report; to noise abroad.

def: adj. Reported, rumoured, noised abroad, famed.
s.f. Naval forces, the navy
s.f. = افيم afīm, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = افيمی afīmī, q.v.

ety: imperat. & v.n. of afzūdan; see afzūd
def: adj. Increasing (used in compn.).

ety: imperat. & v.n. of afzūdan; see afzūd
def: adj. Increasing (used in compn.).
s.f. Act of increasing; increase, addition.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افزایش (afzāyiš).

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of فاضل fāẓil), More excellent; most excellent; preëminent; best; highest
s.m. pl. (of فضل faẓl), Favours; virtues, graces, accomplishments.
s.f. Excellence, preëminence.

ety: afzā, q.v. anda = Zend añṭ = S. anta
def: part. adj. Increasing; that which increases (used in compn.).

ety: v.n. of afzūdan = Zend aiwi+rt. śu; S. abhi+śu
def: s.m. Increase, addition, enlargement; abundance; — adj. More, greater, additional, superfluous, in excess
subs: (-se), To be more (than), to exceed, surpass, &c.

ety: abst. s. fr. afzūda, perf. part. of afzūdan
def: s.f. Increase, addition.
adj. Increasing; more; greater; manifold, much
subs: v.n. To increase, enlarge; to rise (in price).

def: s.f. Increase, enlargement; abundance; rise (in price).
s.m. = آك āk, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀺 (aggi), from Sanskrit अग्नि (agní), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hagníš, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁n̥gʷnis. Doublet of اَگَن (agan) (semi-learned) and اَگْنِی (agnī) (learned).

def: s.f. Fire; flame; heat, excessive heat; (met.) anger, passion; love; lust; hatred; jealousy; intense hunger; venereal disease, syphilis; — adj. Hot as fire, very hot; fiery, hot-tempered, irritable; sharp, quick; scarce, dear
subs: āg uṭhā-rakhnā (-meṅ), To create or cause a quarrel (among or between), to raise a disturbance; to enrage, provoke
v.n. To be exceedingly enraged ( = āg honā)
(-kī), To extinguish fire; to quench the thirst (of); to satisfy hunger, lust, appetite, &c.; to appease a tumult, pacify a quarrel, still resentment

(-par), To rain fire (upon), to pour in shot and shell, to bombard, cannonade; — āg barasnā, v.n. To be very hot; to be parched up; (hence) to become scarce and dear (fire, as it were, raining upon, -par, a thing, and parching it, and so, causing a scarcity); falling in abundance of shot and shell
(-kī), To have the fire of anger, hunger, &c. kindled, to be enraged; to be hungry, &c.
(or babūlā) honā, v.n. To be in a whirlwind of passion, to be mad with rage
(-kī), To stir or blow the fire (of), to fan the flame (of); to stir up, stimulate, excite
To eat fire; to exaggerate greatly; to fabricate, invent; to lie grossly; to boast enormously
Antagonism (such as that) of fire and water; natural antagonism; mortal enmity
v.n. To be annoyed (by), enraged (at); to feel the burning (of heat, grief, hunger, &c.), to be very hot, &c. = āg barasnā, q.v.
v.n. To be blown into a flame; to feel great heat; to be greatly excited, be worked into a fury, get into a towering passion
Antagonism (like that) of fire and straw, &c. = āg-pānī kā bair, q.v.
v.t. To blow the fire, fan the flame; to excite, inflame; to burn excessively, feel on fire
To throw water on the fire, to extinguish the fire; to quench thirst, &c. = āg bujhānā, q.v.
To roll on fire; to be uneasy or restless; to be consumed with jealousy, envy, &c.
To shake off the ashes from a live coal; to strike fire (from a flint, &c.)
v.t. To set fire (to), apply a light or torch (to); to burn, consume, destroy
(-par or meṅ), To set fire (to), to burn
To kindle or make a fire; to inflame, excite sedition, foment a quarrel
v.n. To recover from a violent fit of temper, to cool down
s.m. An extremely active and energetic person; a man of quick apprehension, a sharp fellow
s.m. A spark of fire; a piece of fire, a burning coal
To light or make a fire; to make (a thing) exceedingly warm; to excite anger or envy (com. used among women)
(-meṅ), To sow discord, &c., see āg lagānā
Lit. 'To run for water after setting fire to'; to make a show of curing an evil caused by ooneself; to pretend to appease a quarrel which one has purposely excited; to play tricks, deceive

(-ko, or -meṅ), To apply fire (to), set on fire; to burn, consume; to kindle the fire of strife, sow dissension, make mischief, cause a quarrel, create a disturbance; to inflame, excite, enrage; to eat up, consume; to squander, waste, ruin, destroy; to occasion loss (to); to reduce to poverty; to make a grand display, to do wonders; to treat as worthless or vile, to cast aside, throw or put away, abandon; to discard, disown; to treat with scorn, to scorn, despise; to pay too high a price, to buy dear; to swindle, cheat, pilfer
adj. & s.m. Combustible, inflammable; an incendiary; a seditious person, firebrand, instigator of quarrels

or lag-jānā, v.n. To be set on fire, to be on fire; to catch fire; to burn, be hot; to smart (as a wound); to burn with rage, indignation, envy, jealousy, &c., to be enraged or incensed; to be consumed with love, or grief; to feel intense hunger; to perish, wither, be blighted; to become scarce and dear
intj. May it be burned! perish! blast thee! a murrain seize thee! plague take it!

Lit. 'To look for a cat's urine when (the house) is on fire': to go in quest of small and rarely-found remedies to meet a present and great danger; to put off, delay or excuse oneself on vain pretences
To dig a well after fire has broken out; to shut the stable-door after the horse is stolen
Lit. 'To come for fire'; to pay a flying visit, to pay a doctor's visit
(kisī-kī or g̠air-kī), To bring reproach upon; to accuse; to suffer for another
(-se), To strike fire or a light (from flint, &c)
To become fire; to become very hot; to take fire, to burn with rage, be enraged.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀺 (aggi), from Sanskrit अग्नि (agní), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hagníš, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁n̥gʷnis. Doublet of اَگَن (agan) (semi-learned) and اَگْنِی (agnī) (learned).

def: s.m. Lord, chief, master ( = آقا āqā); a Mog̠al, — generally a Mog̠al trader from Kābul.
wety: From Classical Persian آغا (āġā), from Turkic. Compare Turkish ağa and Bengali আগা (aga).

def: s.m. Front, fore-part, fore-ground; forehead; countenance, face; the private parts; space in front of a house; fore-part of a turband; head or fore-part of a ship or boat; van, vanguard; first charge or assault, onset; that which is gone before, the past (time); that which is before, the future, the morrow, hereafter
subs: Lit. 'To be heavy in the front'; to be pregnant; to be rough, uneven or dangerous (a road)
s.m. Front and stern, the private parts (of the body); the cloth forming the fore and back part of a jāma from the neck to the waist; hesitation, vacillation
To look before and behind; to be cautious, to take care, consider well
To consider well the past and the future, &c., to think about, reflect, deliberate
(-kā), To go after (one), hang about (one), dog one's steps; to hesitate, waver, vacillate, boggle, demur, stand shilly-shally
(-kā), To look after, attend to; to question; to call to account
āgā lenā (-kā), To go forwards, to proceed, advance; to come forward; to take the lead; to bark (a dog) on the approach of a stranger
(-kā), To attack or strike in front; to attack the vanguard (of an army).

def: s.m. Lit. 'unable to go or move'; a mountain; a tree.
adj. Free from disease, healthy; — s.m. Health; a medicine, medicament, drug.
adj. Bottomless, unfathomable, very deep.
adj. Bottomless, unfathomable, very deep.
s.f. Extreme or great depth, unfathomability.

ety: Pehl. ākāś, Zend ākaś, S. आ+काश
def: adj. Informed or apprized (of), acquainted (with, -se), privy (to); knowing, informed, intelligent, versed, conversant
subs: adj. Vigilant, of attentive mind; intelligent, wise
(-ko, of person, and -se, of thing), To inform (a person, of a thing, to acquaint (with), apprize (of), to signify; to report; to caution, forewarn; to give notice, notify.

def: adj. = āgāh, q.v.
s.f. Information, knowledge, intelligence, acquaintance, cognizance; vigilance.
s.f. = āgāhī, q.v.
s.m. The first sheaves of the crop presented to the zamīndār.
s.m. A disease which affects the rice plant (parching it up).
adv. Before, in front, &c.; s.m. Front part, &c. (see aglā).
s.m.f. Bar (of a door or window), bolt.
adv. Up to the neck.
adv. On this side and that, right and left
subs: adj. Pertaining to this side and that, &c.

def: v.t. To put on one side, put aside or away.
adj. Inaccessible, unapproachable, unattainable, incomprehensible; impassable, impenetrable, impervious; bottomless, unfathomable; deep, unfordable; unlimited, unbounded.

s.m. Coming, approach, advent; entrance; acquisition; futurity, the future, the next world; a śāstra containing spells and incantations dictated by Mahādeva; a śāstra or work on sacred science; the Vedas; the East; (in law), voucher, document
subs: v.n. To determine the future, to fore-tell, prophesy
(āgam-vidyā), s.f. The art or science of prognosticating, prognostication, divination, augury, fortune-telling

s.m. Prognosticator; one who tells the doctrines of the Agam = āgam-jānī, āgam jnānī (or gyānī), s.m. Prognosticator, fore-teller, prophet, diviner, augur, fortune-teller; soothsayer; one versed in the tantras.

def: s.m. Coming, approach, advent; entrance; acquisition; futurity, the future, the next world; a śāstra containing spells and incantations dictated by Mahādeva; a śāstra or work on sacred science; the Vedas; the East; (in law), voucher, document
subs: v.n. To determine the future, to fore-tell, prophesy
(āgam-vidyā), s.f. The art or science of prognosticating, prognostication, divination, augury, fortune-telling

s.m. Prognosticator; one who tells the doctrines of the Agam = āgam-jānī, āgam jnānī (or gyānī), s.m. Prognosticator, fore-teller, prophet, diviner, augur, fortune-teller; soothsayer; one versed in the tantras.

def: s.m. Coming, arriving; approach, advent; a proceeding; origin; sexual intercourse.
adj. Coming, ensuing, about to come, future.
adj. Pertaining to the future; — s.m. Prophet, prognosticator, &c. = āgam-jānī, q.v.s.v. āgam.
adj. = agam, q.v.
s.m. Fire, &c. = āg, q.v.
subs: s.m. The south-east; the god of fire and regent of the south-east quarter
s.m. Arrow of fire, rocket, any fiery missile
s.f. The farcy in horses; an eruptive disease in men and elephants; erysipelas
s.m. Any vessel in which fire is kept
s.m. Any stone producing fire, flint
s.m. A certain demon whose body is of fire
s.f. The three sacred fires of the Hindūs, taken collectively, viz. the southern, the household, and the sacrificial fire
s.m. Funeral ceremonies, such as burning a dead body; any ceremony performed with consecrated fire
s.m. Fire-pit, a hollow for kindling fire in
agni-maṇi, s.m. The sun-stone, a fabulous gem supposed to contain and to impart solar heat
s.m. A ceremony consisting in oblations to consecrated fire; the consecrated fire itself
s.m. One who keeps up a perpetual sacrificial fire; a sacrificing priest conversant with the Rigveda, one who performs the ceremony of Hom ( = hom-karnewālā); a fire-worshipper; a sect of Brāhmans
s.m. Chamber of the sacrificial fire
s.m. Sacrifice by fire, burnt sacrifice: offering oblations to fire.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit अग्नि (agni, “fire”). Doublet of آگ (āg). Compare Bengali আগুন (agun).

def: adj. Not reckoned or counted; incalculable, innumerable.
s.m. = اگهار aghār, q.v.
s.m. Dwelling, house, room; receptacle.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आगार (āgāra).

def: conj. If, if so be, in case, in the event of.
ex: اَگَرْ مَیں نے یَہ کِیَا ہوتَا تو ...
agar ma͠i-ne ye kiyā hotā to
If I had done this, then ...
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अगरु (agaru).

def: s.m. = آگار āgār, q.v.
s.m. Salt-mine, salt-pit; — adj. Full of; competent, versed in, proficient.
s.m. Wood of aloes, Aquillaria agallocha; — the name of a certain tree which produces bdellium, Amyris agallocha; — the sisu tree, Dalbergia sisoo
subs: s.m. A vessel for holding agar or perfumes; a scent-bottle.
ex: اَگَرْ مَیں نے یَہ کِیَا ہوتَا تو ...
agar ma͠i-ne ye kiyā hotā to
If I had done this, then ...
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अगरु (agaru).

def: s.m. = اگهاڙا aghāṛā, q.v.
s.m. = اگڙبگڙ agaṛ-bagaṛ, q.v.
s.m. Confusior, jumble, medley, mess, hash; odds and ends; trifles, trumpery; trash, nonsense; — adj. Mixed, confused, jumbled; promiscuous, composed of odds and ends.
conj. Although, though, even if, granted that.
s.f. Money, pay, &c. given in advance.
s.f. = آگا āgā, q.v.; money, &c. paid in advance, advances; onset, first charge; the fore-fetters of a horse; — adv. In front, before; forward, onward, further on, in advance (see āge)
subs: s.f. The front and heel ropes with which a horse is fastened: agāṛī mārnā (-kī), To attack in front; to attack the advanced guard; to defeat (an enemy).

def: s.m. A manufacturer of salt (āgar, q.v.).
(Gr. ἀγαρικὸν Arabicized), s. Common agaric, Boletus igniarius.
s.m. = انگرکها angarkhā, q.v.
= next, q.v.
s.m. A race of merchants of the Vaisya tribe (from Agrohā, to the west of Dehlī); — the descendants of Rājā Agar.
adj. = agraʼī, q.v.
s.m. A manufacturer of salt (āgar, q.v.).
s.m. = آکاس ākās, q.v. (The word appears to be peculiar to Southern India.)
s.m. = آکاس ākās, q.v. (The word appears to be peculiar to Southern India.)

ety: perf. part. of āg̠ashtan; ā+S. rt. gar or ghar
def: part. adj. Moistened, macerated; mixed, polluted.
s.f. A turband; a terrace in front of an upper room; portico; verandah.
s.m. August (the month).
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese agosto, influenced by English August.. Compare Konkani आगोस्त (āgosta), Marathi आगस्त (āgasta).

def: s.m. Name of a tree, Ӕschynomene grandiflora; the star Canopus, (also) the name of the regent of that star.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese agosto, influenced by English August.. Compare Konkani आगोस्त (āgosta), Marathi आगस्त (āgasta).

def: s.m. The name of a small tribe of Rajpūts.
s.m. Name of a tree, Ӕschynomene grandiflora; the star Canopus, (also) the name of the regent of that star.
s.f. Future, futurity, the hereafter ( = āgā, q.v.).
part. adj. Arrived, come; present; — s.m. A guest.
s.f. Income; wealth, stock, property; respect, courtesy, attention, consideration (see next).
s.m. A butcher's stall. (The word appears to be peculiar to Southern India.)
s.f. Arrival; concern.

adj. Without character, dishonoured, disgraced; — s.m.f. One whose funeral ceremonies have not been performed; — s.f. Distress, want of resource, necessity, inconvenience; disgrace, dishonour; condemnation, damnation.
v.t. To bring together, collect, assemble.
adj. Bodiless, incorporeal.
adj. Coming, arriving, approaching; future.
s.m. Concern.
s.m. Advance, &c. (see āga and agāṛī); — adv. In advance, in front, before, beforehand
subs: v.n. To advance in order to meet a person coming on a visit.

def: s.m. The top of the sugar-cane cut up for seed.
s.f. = agwānī, q.v.
s.m. An advance of rent paid by the cultivator to the zamīndār in the months of jeṭh and āsāṛh (cf. agāṛī)
subs: s.f. A division of the crop in predetermined proportions between landlord and tenant; a watching and sharing, each party keeping watch (see next) over the fields to prevent the crop being fraudulently carried off.

def: s.f. = agaur, q.v.
s.m. Beginning, commencement
subs: v.t. To make a beginning, to begin
v.n. To commence, begin to be.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آغَاز (āġāz).

def: s.m. = اگڙا agṛā, q.v.
adj. Firm, strong; resolute, brave, unflinching.
intj. (used to a female of the lower order) O! holla! (the word appears to be peculiar to Southern and Western India).

adv. ( = agāṛī and āgū), Before, in front, in the presence of, confronting, facing, opposite, in view, in sight; in the time or reign; in advance; foremost; fore, beyond, onward, further, further on, furthermore, more than this; in future, hereafter, henceforth; again; for the future; next in time or place, then, afterwards; thereupon, after that; formerly, in former times; already
subs: adv. (intensive or emphat. of āge), in advance, before, ahead; later on, by and by, hereafter

v.n. To come forward, come in sight; to advance, approach, draw near; to confront, face, defy, challenge; to come between, interpose; to come to pass, happen, occur, befall, betide; to come upon (retributively); to return, revert; to reap what one has sown; to come to light, be discovered

v n. To go before, advance, go forward or ahead; to make head or progress, push on, get along; to go forward to meet and escort a visitor; to go beyond, surpass, outstrip, excel; to come forward, face, confront, oppose; to challenge; to lead, show the way

adv. One after another, successively, in succession, in a line; before and behind, front and rear, fore and aft; again, hereafter, henceforth, for the future; sooner or later; in the absence of, behind one's back; on fitting occasion or opportunity; straggling, irregularly; in inverted order, transposed
v.t. To place, lay or put before; to present, offer; to make (one) go before, keep (one) in sight; to put forward
adv. Looking before; with the eyes open; carefully, with circumspection
v.n. To look before, keep the eyes open, look about one, take care, exercise caution or circumspection; to look to the future
v.t. To throw or place before; to supply with the means of support
adv. Before-hand, in anticipation, previously, before: (in phrases it may often be conveniently rendered by the prefix pre-, as āge-se ṭhānnā, To predetermine, presume, preconceive
( = agāṛū rakhnā), v.t. To put forward; to place or lay before, &c. = āge dhar-lenā, q.v.
s.m. Dependant, follower, servant; parasite, hanger-on, sycophant
v.t. To bring forward, adduce, produce; to place, lay or set before
adv. Further on (in time or space), in future; ahead, subsequently
v.t. To bring forward, advance, to bring before
v.n. To go before, advance, &c. ( = āge baṛhnā); to be forward or bold; to face (one) boldly, to be rude or impertinent.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa अग्गइ (aggaï), from Sanskrit अग्रके (agrake).

def: s.f. = angeṭhī, q.v.
s.m.f. Bar (of a door or window), bolt.
s.m. A race of merchants of the Vaisya tribe (from Agrohā, to the west of Dehlī); — the descendants of Rājā Agar.
s.m. The first morsel.
s.f. Command, order, injunction, precept, instruction, direction; provision, requisition; permission, leave, authority; edict, decree; (in Gram.) the imperative mood
subs: (-kī), To observe or obey the orders (of)
s.m. Written order or authority, warrant, commission, edict
aggyā-kārī, adj. Executing command, acting according to orders; obedient, submissive, dutiful
v.t. To give, or issue, an order (to, -ko), to command, bid, instruct, direct; to permit, allow; to decide, settle; to do the bidding (of, -kī), to obey, submit (to)
v.t. To keep under authority, subjection, or control; to rule, govern
(-kī), To be under the order or control or authority (of), to be subject (to), to obey, submit (to); to be docile
v.t. 'To bring under authority, control or subjection, to subdue, master
(S. ājnā+anusāra), adv. & postpn. In accordance with the command or bidding (of, -kī).

def: s.f. Command, order, injunction, precept, instruction, direction; provision, requisition; permission, leave, authority; edict, decree; (in Gram.) the imperative mood
subs: (-kī), To observe or obey the orders (of)
s.m. Written order or authority, warrant, commission, edict
aggyā-kārī, adj. Executing command, acting according to orders; obedient, submissive, dutiful
v.t. To give, or issue, an order (to, -ko), to command, bid, instruct, direct; to permit, allow; to decide, settle; to do the bidding (of, -kī), to obey, submit (to)
v.t. To keep under authority, subjection, or control; to rule, govern
(-kī), To be under the order or control or authority (of), to be subject (to), to obey, submit (to); to be docile
v.t. 'To bring under authority, control or subjection, to subdue, master
(S. ājnā+anusāra), adv. & postpn. In accordance with the command or bidding (of, -kī).

def: adj. Ignorant, inexperienced, unknowing, foolish, stupid, idiotic; unknown; — adv. Unwittingly, &c.; — s.m. An ignorant person, &c.; — a stranger.
s.m. Ignorance foolishness, folly, stupidity
adj. Not knowing, unknowing, ignorant, inexperienced; thoughtless, inconsiderate, foolish; — s.m. An ignorant person, &c.
(S. ajnānatwam), s.m. = H اگيانتا अज्ञानता aggyāntā (S. ajnānatā),s.f. Ignorance, folly, &c. ( = aggyān).
(S. ajnānatā), s.f. = H اگيانپن अज्ञानपन aggyānpan (S. ajnānatwam), s.m. Ignorance, folly, &c. ( = aggyān).
part. adj. Unknown, not understood; ignorant, inexperienced, simple
subs: (S. ajnāta+yauvanā), s.f. A girl arrived at maturity and surprised at the symptoms which then appear, not knowing to what to attribute her sensations.

def: s.f. Ignorance, &c. ( = aggyān).
s.m. Sin, guilt, crime, wickedness; suffering
subs: adj. & s.m. Sin-destroying, freeing from sin, purifying; — destroyer of sin
s.m. Remover of guilt, — an epithet of the Deity.

def: s.f. Surfeit, satiety, repletion, fulness; — adv. To the full or utmost; severely, harshly, with a strong or heavy hand.
adj. Satiated, surfeited, satisfied, full; — s.m. A rich man, one who enjoys a competency.
s.m. The name of the eighth month of the luni-solar year of the Hindūs (Nov.-Dec.).
v.n. To be surfeited or satiated (with, -se), to be full, satisfied, contented; to swell with pride, give oneself airs
adj. Relating to the month Aghan; — s.f. The produce of that portion of the ḵẖarīf crop which is gathered in the month Aghan, the harvest of the cold season
s.m. Greediness, covetousness, avarice.
s.m. Placing sacred food, or sprinkling clarified butter, before an idol; sprinkling ghī upon the fire at certain sacrifices; ghī or clarified butter.
s.m. The plant Achyranthes aspera (said to cure the bite of venomous reptiles; — syn. ćirćirā).
adj. Greedy, covetous, avaricious (cf. aghorī); s.m. A miser.
s.m. Land held in perpetuity and not alienable.
s.m. Beating; stroke, blow; cast, throw; gust; killing, slaughter; place of execution, slaughter-house.
s.m. Beating; stroke, blow; cast, throw; gust; killing, slaughter; place of execution, slaughter-house.
adj. Not wounded, unscathed, sound, whole.
adj. Unfit, unworthy; — s.m. The unknown future.
adj. Satiated, surfeited, satisfied, full; — s.m. A rich man, one who enjoys a competency.
s.m. Lemon grass (see agyā-ghās, s.v. agyā).
s.m. A title of the god Śiva; — adj. Terrible, formidable
subs: s.m. An order of religious mendicants, among Hindūs, who worship Śiva: (They eat anything and everything, however filthy or unclean, even human carcases; — hence) a gross or filthy feeder; a dirty-looking man
s.m. A member of the order aghor-panth; glutton, &c. ( = the next, q.v.).

def: adj. & s.m. ( = aghor-panthī), Filthy, foul, impure, unclean; — a glutton, gormandizer.
s.m. Filthiness, foulness, uncleanness, impurity; greediness, covetousness.
s.m. Filthiness, foulness, uncleanness, impurity; greediness, covetousness.
adj. Without noise or sound; (in Gram.) hard.
s.f. Fire, &c. ( = āg, q.v.)
adj. Future; possible. (Cf. aglā.)
s.m. Enclosure for sheep, sheepcote.
adj. Full (used in comp.)
s.m. Name of a bird, a species of lark ( = agiyā, q.v.).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit अग्नि (agni, “fire”). Doublet of آگ (āg). Compare Bengali আগুন (agun).

def: s.m. Name of a bird, a species of lark, Alauda aggia (also called agin).
s.f. Fire, &c. = āg, q.v.
subs: s.m. A demon who rules over fire
s.f. Lemon-grass, Andropogon schœnanthus (also called aghin- or agin-ghās).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Foremost, first, chief, principal; prior, previous, preceding, former, anterior; late, recent, belonging to the past, old; coming, approaching, next, subsequent, forthcoming, future; — s.m. The first, the foremost (person or thing), the chief, the best; the preceding, hence, (in the pl. agle), elders, ancients, forefathers, ancestors, predecessors; the living, the present generation; posterity, descendants, succeeding generations; another person, a second person ( = dūsrā, but generally implying a senior or a superior); husband, partner (husband or wife); the Supreme Being; supreme lord, tyrant, oppressor; the opposite party, opponent, adversary (in a lawsuit, or a match); the privities
subs: s.m. Former birth or life; the future, the next world
s.f. The fruits of one's acts in this life, or in a former existence, according to Hindū belief
pron. They, those; — adv. Formerly, previously ( = S. agre, and H. āge)
s.m. Ancestors, elders, predecessors; descendants, posterity; preceding and succeeding generations
adj. Belonging to a former age or generation; simple, artless, innocent, guileless, plain, straight-forward.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀮 (aggala), from Sanskrit अग्र (ágra, “front; top”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀮- (-la-).

def: adj. & adv. (compar. & superl. of غالب g̠ālib), More, or most, likely; most probable; very probably
subs: It is most, or very probable (that, -ki).

def: s.m. pl. (of غل g̠ull), Yokes, chains (for the neck).
s.m. The advanced guard; the East; — adj. Adventurous, venturesome; forward, early (as fruit, &c.).
s.f. = agwānī, q.v.
s.m. = angnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀕𑁆𑀕𑁂𑀇 (uggei), from Sanskrit *उद्गाति (*udgāti). Cognate with Gujarati ઉગવું (ugvũ), Punjabi اُگّنا (uggnā) / ਉੱਗਣਾ (uggaṇā), Sindhi اُڳڻ (ug̠ṇ) / उॻणु (ug̠aṇu).

def: s.m. Anything appertaining to fire, or the deity of fire; the south-east quarter; — adj. Fiery, pungent, piquant, stimulating, digestive; — south-eastern.
s.f. = اگن agni, q.v.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अग्नि (agní). Doublet of آگ (āg) and اَگَن (agan).

def: s.m. An insect of scarlet colour the lady-bird.
s.m. pl. (of غني g̠anī), The rich; independent people.
adj. Not obvious, imperceptible, invisible; unseen, unperceived; hidden, secret, mysterious; — s.m. The invisible Supreme Being.
v.t. = agornā, q.v.
s.m. Watchman, guard; one who watches over crops.
s.m. Watchman, guard; one who watches over crops.
s.f. Advance of money, &c. (see agāṛī and agaur); adv. Before-hand, in advance.
= agoṛī, q.v.
s.m. = agoṛ, q.v.
v.t. To watch, guard, take care of.
s.f. Embrace; bosom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آغوش (āġōš), from Middle Persian [script needed] (ʾkwš /⁠āgōš⁠/, “embrace”).

def: adj. Of a different family or tribe.
adj. Front, foremost; first, prior, anterior; chief, prominent, best; — s.m. Foremost point or part, forepart, front, tip, uppermost part
subs: s.m. The first, chief, front or foremost part of anything
adj. Of prior birth, firstborn; — s.m. An elder brother
adj. Preceding; s.m. Chief, leader, guide
adj. Forecasting, provident, cautious; — s.m. One endued with foresight; a cautious man
s.f. The advance guard of an army
s.m. The eighth month in the lunar year of the Hindūs (Nov.-Dec.), when the moon is full near the head of Orion.
ex: اَگَرْ مَیں نے یَہ کِیَا ہوتَا تو ...
agar ma͠i-ne ye kiyā hotā to
If I had done this, then ...
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अगरु (agaru).

def: s.m. The city of Agra (called also Akbar-ābād)
subs: (or āgre-wārā), adj. (f. -ī), Of or pertaining to Agra; — s.m. A native, or a resident, of Agra.

def: s.m. An ear of corn or rice which has been blighted and contains no grain.
s.m. Seizing, taking; surpassing, surmounting; favour, patronage; ability, power; pertinacity
subs: v.t. To seize, &c.; to hold tenaciously (to); to insist.

def: adj. Not to be received or taken, unacceptable.
adj. Of the colour of aloe wood (agar); of or relating to agar (q.v.)
s.m. Confusior, jumble, medley, mess, hash; odds and ends; trifles, trumpery; trash, nonsense; — adj. Mixed, confused, jumbled; promiscuous, composed of odds and ends.
s.m. A village, or the quarter of a town or village, inhabited by Brāhmans.
s.m. Food offered in oblations, sacrifices, &c. to the gods.
s.m. Food offered in oblations, sacrifices, &c. to the gods.
s.f. Priority, precedence.
s.m. A vessel for holding perfumes (agar); a scent-bottle ( = agar-dān).
s.m. pl. (of غرض g̠araẓ), Motives, aims, &c. (see g̠araẓ).
adj. First in order or rank, chief, eldest; subsequent, future; — adv. Before, prior; subsequently: — s.m. An elder brother.
s.m. Eruptions from heat on the tongue and lips; the thrush, aphthœ. (The word seems peculiar to Southern and Western India.)
s.f. The Agati tree = اکتي aktī, q.v.
adv. = اگے āge, q.v.
s.m. Pommel (of a saddle).
adj. Unskilful; without merit; ineffectual; bad; void of qualities (good or bad); — an epithet of the Deity; — s.m. Bad quality or property, defect, fault, vice.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit अग्नि (agni, “fire”). Doublet of آگ (āg). Compare Bengali আগুন (agun).

def: adj. Not hidden, unconcealed.
adj. Easy of comprehension, evident, manifest
subs: adj. Of an ingenuous disposition, open, honest, candid.

def: s.m. Thumb-ring (cf. angūṭhā and angushtāna).
s.m. Guide, leader, conductor, forerunner, harbinger; one who negotiates a marriage, a match-maker; the opening in a (Hindū) woman's skirt.
s.f. Burning, conflagration.
s.f. Burning, conflagration.

s.m. Guide, leader, fore-runner, harbinger ( = agwā); agent, manager; — s.f. Guidance, direction, leadership; reception, welcome, going or sending forward to meet and receive a visitor with honour ( = agaunī; syn. istiqbāl)
subs: (-kī), To advance to meet the bridegroom at a wedding, or a visitor on the road; to escort to one's home.

def: s.m. Guide, leader, fore-runner, harbinger ( = agwā); agent, manager; — s.f. Guidance, direction, leadership; reception, welcome, going or sending forward to meet and receive a visitor with honour ( = agaunī; syn. istiqbāl)
subs: (-kī), To advance to meet the bridegroom at a wedding, or a visitor on the road; to escort to one's home.

def: s.m. A portion of corn set apart for village servants (like the customary 'sharping corn' in England); the perquisite of the ploughman in kind, rent, &c., paid in advance.
s.m. Front, fore-part; space in front of a house
subs: s.m. Places in front and rear, neighbourhood; persons living in front and rear (of), neighbours.

def: s.f. The body of a ploughshare.
v.t. To burn vessels of metal for the purpose of cleaning them.
s.m. pl. (of غير g̠air), Unknown persons, strangers, foreigners; rivals.
s.f. The state of being a stranger, &c., strangeness; rivalry. (The word seems to be peculiar to Southern India.)
s.f. Kindling the fire at the time of devotion (by Hindūs; — see hom).
s.f. Egotism, self-conceit, pride, self-sufficiency.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इह (ihá), doublet of یَہاں (yahā̃).

def: s.f. Difficulty, trouble, trial.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इह (ihá), doublet of یَہاں (yahā̃).

def: s.m. A day.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इह (ihá), doublet of یَہاں (yahā̃).

def: s.f. Sigh; — intj. Ah! alas!
subs: āh karnā, āh khaiṅćnā, āh mārnā, āh nikālnā, To draw, heave, or bring forth a sigh; to sigh
intj. Ah! oh! alas! ah me!

def: intj. Ah! alas! oh dear! heigho! (exclamation of pain, pity, &c.); — Ha! O! holloa! bravo! capital! how excellent! well done! strange! wonderful! how extraordinary!
intj. Ah! alas! oh dear! heigho! (exclamation of pain, pity, &c.); — Ha! O! holloa! bravo! capital! how excellent! well done! strange! wonderful! how extraordinary!
adj. More, or most, beloved; most, or very, lovely; very amiable: — s.m. A friend.
s.m. Unity; one; an individual
subs: One of the two parties (in a law-suit)
One of the two suitors (in court).

def: s.m. Units; cardinal numbers.

s.m. A body of Indian veterans of the time of the Emperor Akbar, somewhat of the nature of pensioners, but liable to be called out for active service on emergency; (hence) one who is supported by the king as a pensioner, without rendering service of any kind; (hence) a lazy, indolent fellow.
s.f. (pl. of ḥadīs̤), Traditions, particularly traditions or sayings of Moḥammad, as constituting the basis of the sunnat or traditional law.
s.f. Unity, the being singular; individuality; — concord; alliance.
intj. = ahā, q.v.
s.m. Quicklime ( = ćūnā); cement, plaster.
adj. More, or most, proper or deserving; very proper; very deserving.
s.m. Freshness of soil (cf. āhlād, āhlānā, ahle-gahle).

r: ahālī, ahl, ahālī-mawālī, ahālī o mawālī
def: s.m. People at large, poor and rich; courtiers; retainers, train, retinue, followers, dependents.
s.f. Name of the wife of Gautama, q.v.; — name of an Apsarā.
pron. I
subs: adj. Knowing the ego; — s.m. Self-consciousness; one who possesses self-knowledge
s.f. Self-illusion, spiritual ignorance; self-sufficiency, self-love, conceit, arrogance, pride.

def: adj. compar. & superl. (rt. همّ), More, most, or very important; momentous
intj. No! nay! do not! by no means ( = unhuṅ).
adv. No, nay, &c. = ahāṅ, q.v.

ety: Pehl. aśin, Zend. ayaṅh, S. अयस्
def: s.m. Iron
subs: s.f. The work or trade of a blacksmith.

def: s.m. Chopped straw mixed with mud (used for building).
s.m. Design, purpose, intention; method, manner; sound, concord, melody; one of the Persian tunes or modulations in music.

ety: āhan, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Of iron, iron-, made of iron
subs: adj. Iron-fisted, having an iron grip, strong, powerful.

ety: āhan, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Of iron, iron-, made of iron
subs: adj. Iron-fisted, having an iron grip, strong, powerful.

def: s.m. Making much of self, thinking of self, egotism, individuality; self-consciousness; self-sufficiency, pride, haughtiness, arrogance.
adj. Egotistical, self-sufficient, self-conceited, proud, arrogant; — s.m. A self-conceited person, &c.
adj. = ahaṅkārī, q.v.
s.m. Gluten, starch, size, paste, glue (see next)
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
s.m. Taking food, eating, aliment, sustenance, food, victuals
subs: (-ko), To feed, eat, consume, make a meal.

def: s.m. Reservoir for collecting rain-water for irrigation, pond; — pile of cakes of cowdung in a furnace, fuel made of cowdung.
adj. Taking food, eating; — s.m. Eater.
s.f. Coming and going; ingress and egress ( = ānā-jānā, āwā-jāwā, q.q.v.).
v.t. To starch, paste, &c. = ahār ćaṛhānā, q.v.s.v ahār.
adv. Day and night, the whole day; — during the whole day, continually.
adj. Unhappy, sorrowful.
s.f. Sound of feet approaching, sound of soft footsteps; sound, noise, clack, tick
subs: To be on the qui vive for the sound of footsteps, &c.; to be on the watch, to be on the alert.

def: s.m. Friends, lovers; dear ones.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَحْبَاب (ʔaḥbāb), plural of حِبّ (ḥibb) (whence حبیب (habīb)).

def: s.m. = āhvān, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of عہد 'to enjoin'; 'to make a compact,' &c.
def: s.m. Injunction, charge, mandate; will, testament; — compact, contract, covenant, agreement, engagement, obligation, promise; bond, league, treaty; — a vow, an oath; — time, season, conjuncture; lifetime; reign (of a king)
subs: To break an engagement, or promise, &c.
s.m. Holder of a contract; an officer (of the Mogul government) who engaged to collect the revenue of a district for a small percentage
adj. & s.m. Promise-breaking, faithless to (one's) word or engagement; — a promise-breaker, one who is faithless to his engagement, &c.
(vulg. -shiknī), s.f. Breach of contract; breach of faith; infraction of a treaty
v.n. To engage, to contract, to promise, to agree; to make a vow, to vow; to abjure
s.m. A contract, bond, an agreement; a treaty, convention; a charter; a will; a diploma

s.m. Articles of agreement, &c.; a treaty of alliance; — ʻahd-o-paimān karnā, v.n. To contract (with, -se), to covenant, to bargain; to enter into a convention, make a treaty, make terms or conditions (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عهد ('ahd), itself borrowed from Arabic عَهْد (ʕahd).

ety: inf. n. iv of حدث 'to exist newly'
def: Bringing into existence; causing to be made; making or constructing afresh; production; invention; novelty; innovation.

ety: inf. n. iv of حدث 'to exist newly'
def: Bringing into existence; causing to be made; making or constructing afresh; production; invention; novelty; innovation.
intj. O! sign of the voc. case ( = eh, ai, he).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इह (ihá), doublet of یَہاں (yahā̃).

def: intj. O! holloa!
s.m. = aheṛ, q.v.
